utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi logan . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hey , good to see you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: it's good to see you as well .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so i know the nurse told you about dax .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'd like to tell dax a little bit about you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so logan is a 58 year old male , with a past medical history significant for diabetes type 2 , hypertension , osteoarthritis , who presents today with some back pain .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so logan , what happened to your back ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , we were helping my daughter with some heavy equipment and lifted some boxes a little too quickly , and they were a little too heavy .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay ... and did you strain your back , did something-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i thought i heard a pop when i moved and i had to lie down for about an hour before it actually relieved the pain . and then it's been a little stiff ever since . and this was- what , so today's tuesday . this was saturday morning .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , all right .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and is it your lower back , your upper back ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: my lower back .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: your lower back , okay . and what- what have you taken for the pain ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i took some tylenol , i took some ibuprofen , i used a little bit of icy heat on the spot but it really did n't seem to help .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and um ... do you have any numbing or tingling in your legs ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh ... i felt some tingling in my toes on my right foot until about sunday afternoon . and then that seemed to go away .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and is there a position that you feel better in ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh ... it's really tough to find a comfortable spot sleeping at night . i would- i tend to lie on my right side and that seemed to help a little bit ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , all right .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: well , um ... so how are you doing otherwise ? i know that , you know , we have some issues to talk-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . about today . were you able to take any vacations over the summer ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um ... some long weekends , which was great . just kind of- trying to mix it up through the summer . so lots of three day weekends .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , well i'm glad to hear that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um ... so let's talk a little bit about your diabetes . how are you doing with that ? i know that- you know , i remember you have a sweet tooth . so ...

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah ... i-i love peanut butter cups . um ... and i have to say that when we were helping my daughter , we were on the fly and on the go and haven't had a home cooked meal in weeks, our diets were less than stellar .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and uh ... i-i think i need to go clean for a couple of weeks . but other than that , it was been- it's been pretty good eating .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , all right . and how about your high blood pressure ? are you monitoring your blood pressure readings at home , like i recommended ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm good about it during the week while i am at home working, but on the weekends when i'm out of the house i tend to forget . uh , and so it's not as regimented , but it's been pretty good and-and under control for the most part .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and you're you're taking your medication ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i am .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and then lastly , i know that you had had some early arthritis in your knee . how- how are you doing with that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh ... it gets aggravated every once in a while . if i- maybe if i run too much or if i've lift boxes that are a little too heavy , i start to feel the strain . but it's been okay . not great , but it's been okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right , well ... let me go ahead and- you know , i know that the nurse did a review of systems sheet with you when you- when you checked in . i know that you were endorsing the back pain .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: have you had any other symptoms , chest pain , nausea or vomiting-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . fever , chills ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no . no none whatsoever .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no . okay . all right , well let me go ahead , i want to do a quick physical exam .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon ? show me the blood pressure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so it's a little elevated . your blood pressure's a little elevated here in the office , but you know you could be in some pain , which could make your-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . blood pressure go up . let's look at the readings .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon ? show me the blood pressure readings .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah ... yeah you know they do run a little bit on the high side , so we'll have to address that as well .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , well . let me- i'm just going to be listening your heart and your lungs and i'll check out your back and i'll let you know what i find , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and kick against my hands .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , good . all right .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , so ... on physical examination , you know , i-i do hear a slight 2 out of 6 s- s- systolic heart murmur .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: on your heart exam . which you've had in the past .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so that sounds stable to me .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: on your back exam , you know , you do have some pain to palpation of the lumbar spine . and you have pain with flexion and extension of the back . and you have a negative straight leg raise , which is which is good . so , let's- let's just look at some of your results , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon ? show me the diabetes labs .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , so ... in reviewing the results of your diabetes labs , your hemoglobin a1c is a little elevated at eight . i'd like to see it a little bit better , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon ? show me the back x-ray .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so in reviewing the results of your back x-ray , this looks like a normal x-ray . there's good bony alignment , there's normal uh- there's no fracture present . uh , so this is a normal x-ray of your back , which is not surprising based on-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . the history , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so let's just go ahead and we'll- we're going to go over , you know , my assessment and my plan for you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so for your first problem , your back pain . you know , i think you have a lumbar strain from the lifting . so , let's go ahead . we can prescribe you some meloxicam 15 mg once a day .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i want you to continue to ice it , okay . i want you to try to avoid any strenuous activity and we can go ahead and- and refer you to physical therapy-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . and see how you do , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you got it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: for your next problem , your diabetes . y-you know , i think it's a little under- out of control . so i want to increase the metformin to 1000 mg twice a day . and i'm going to um ... um ... i'm going to repeat a hemoglobin a1c in about 6 months , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon ? order a hemoglobin a1c .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , for your third problem , your hypertension . uh ... i-i'd like to go ahead increase the lisinopril from 10 mg to 20 mg a day .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: does that sound okay ? i think we need to get it under better control .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no that's fine . i agree .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon ? order lisinopril 20 mg daily .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and for your last problem , your osteoarthritis , i-i think that you were doing a really good job , in terms of you know what , monitoring your knee and uh ...

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i do n't think we need to do any- any further , you know , work up of that at this time , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: do you have any questions logan ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: not at this point .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so the nurse will come in to help you get checked out , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you got it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon ? finalize the note .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 58-year-old male with diabetes type 2, hypertension, and osteoarthritis presenting with back pain.
The doctor asks about the cause of the patient's back pain.
The patient reports back pain after lifting heavy boxes too quickly while helping his daughter.
The patient heard a pop, had to lie down for an hour, and has experienced stiffness since Saturday.
The doctor asks for the location of the back pain.
The patient reports the pain is in his lower back.
The doctor asks what the patient has taken for the pain.
The patient used Tylenol, ibuprofen, and Icy Hot without relief.
The doctor asks if the patient has any numbness or tingling in his legs.
The patient reports tingling in his right toes that resolved Sunday afternoon.
The doctor asks if any position improves the pain.
The patient finds it difficult to sleep and notes that lying on his right side provides some relief.
Diabetes type 2.
The patient reports poor dietary habits recently due to travel.
The patient intends to improve his diet.
Hypertension.
The doctor asks if the patient is monitoring his blood pressure at home.
The patient monitors blood pressure at home and reports it is generally under control.
The doctor asks if the patient is taking his medication.
The patient confirms medication adherence.
Osteoarthritis.
The patient reports occasional knee aggravation with running or lifting heavy objects.
The doctor asks about chest pain, nausea, vomiting, fevers, and chills.
The patient denies chest pain, nausea, and vomiting.
The patient denies fevers and chills.
The doctor performs a physical examination.
The patient's blood pressure is elevated in the office.
The physical exam reveals a 2/6 systolic ejection murmur.
The back exam shows lumbar spine tenderness, pain with flexion and extension, and a negative straight leg raise.
The patient's hemoglobin A1c is elevated at 8.
The back X-ray is unremarkable with normal alignment and no fractures.
The doctor diagnoses a lumbar strain.
The doctor prescribes meloxicam 15 mg once a day.
The doctor advises icing the area and avoiding strenuous activity.
The doctor refers the patient to physical therapy.
The doctor increases metformin to 1000 mg twice a day.
The doctor orders a repeat hemoglobin A1c in 6 months.
The doctor increases lisinopril from 10 mg to 20 mg a day.
The doctor asks if the patient agrees with the plan to increase lisinopril.
The patient agrees with the treatment plan.
The doctor determines no further workup is needed for osteoarthritis.
The doctor asks if the patient has any questions.
